game_info.title: Temple Escape 3D
game_info.concept:
The Original Endless Running Adventure!

App Store URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/temple-run/id420009108

================================================================================
Full Description:
================================================================================

The Original Endless Running Adventure!
Experience the thrill of Temple Run – the global phenomenon that redefined mobile gaming!
Steal the cursed idol and escape from the relentless Demon Monkeys in a heart-pounding adventure through ancient temples, jungle ruins, and precarious cliff edges.
Outrun danger in the ultimate maze-running, jungle-escaping, temple adventure!

- Swipe to turn, jump, and slide past deadly obstacles
- Race through temples, bridges, and treacherous paths
- Collect coins, unlock diverse characters, and boost your power-ups
- Test your reflexes in a fast and frenzied experience
- Challenge friends & climb the leaderboards

"Temple Run is that heart-pounding action movie moment – but non-stop!” – SlideToPlay
"One of the most thrilling and fun running games ever!" – TheAppera
"A fast and frenzied experience." – IGN
"Very addicting… a truly different running game!" – Appolicious

Join over 50 MILLION players worldwide in this legendary treasure-hunting escape!

Download now and see how far you can run!
game_info.description:
Escape the Cursed Temple! Run for your life along the ancient stone pathways floating in the void.
Dodge deadly traps, leap over crumbling gaps, and slide under hanging ruins.
Collect the Sacred Gold Coins to increase your score while outrunning the encroaching darkness.
How far can you go before the temple claims you?
game_info.controls:
- Arrow Left / A: Move Left Lane
- Arrow Right / D: Move Right Lane
- Arrow Up / W / Space: Jump
- Arrow Down / S: Slide / Duck
- Enter: Start Game
- Esc: Pause / Resume
- R: Restart (when Game Over)

TEST_1.test_description: Verify player initialization and basic forward movement physics.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Start the game in TEST_1 mode. Allow the game to run for 60 frames. Check if the player's Z position decreases (moving forward into the screen) and if the player stays on the ground (Y position stable).
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player Z position should be less than 0. Player Y position should be approximately constant (ground level). Game phase should remain "PLAYING".
TEST_1.description: Verify player initialization and basic forward movement physics.
TEST_1.strategy: Start the game in TEST_1 mode. Allow the game to run for 60 frames. Check if the player's Z position decreases (moving forward into the screen) and if the player stays on the ground (Y position stable).
TEST_1.expected: Player Z position should be less than 0. Player Y position should be approximately constant (ground level). Game phase should remain "PLAYING".

TEST_2.test_description: Verify jumping mechanics and gravity application.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Start game. Simulate a JUMP input at frame 10. Monitor Y position and velocity for 60 frames to ensure ascent and descent back to ground.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player Y should increase after frame 10, reach a peak, and return to ground level. 'onGround' flag should be false during jump and true after landing.
TEST_2.description: Verify jumping mechanics and gravity application.
TEST_2.strategy: Start game. Simulate a JUMP input at frame 10. Monitor Y position and velocity for 60 frames to ensure ascent and descent back to ground.
TEST_2.expected: Player Y should increase after frame 10, reach a peak, and return to ground level. 'onGround' flag should be false during jump and true after landing.

TEST_3.strategy_description: Start game. Trigger Left Move at frame 10, then Right Move at frame 60. Monitor X position target and actual mesh interpolation.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Player X should interpolate from 0 to -LANE_WIDTH, stay there, then interpolate back to 0. Movement should be smooth (lerped).
TEST_3.strategy: Start game. Trigger Left Move at frame 10, then Right Move at frame 60. Monitor X position target and actual mesh interpolation.
TEST_3.expected: Player X should interpolate from 0 to -LANE_WIDTH, stay there, then interpolate back to 0. Movement should be smooth (lerped).

TEST_4.test_description: Verify collision detection with obstacles causes Game Over.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Spawn a guaranteed obstacle immediately in front of the player. Move player forward into it without dodging. Monitor game phase.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Game phase should transition from "PLAYING" to "GAME_OVER_LOSE" upon impact with the obstacle.
TEST_4.description: Verify collision detection with obstacles causes Game Over.
TEST_4.strategy: Spawn a guaranteed obstacle immediately in front of the player. Move player forward into it without dodging. Monitor game phase.
TEST_4.expected: Game phase should transition from "PLAYING" to "GAME_OVER_LOSE" upon impact with the obstacle.

TEST_5.strategy_description: Spawn a coin directly in the player's path. Move player through it. Check score increment.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Score should increase by the coin value. Coin entity should be removed from the active entities list.

TEST_5.expected: Score should increase by the coin value. Coin entity should be removed from the active entities list.

TEST_6.strategy_description: Trigger SLIDE input. Check player height/scale logic and verify state timer for sliding duration.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Player mesh Y scale should decrease or position lower. After slide duration, player should return to normal height.

TEST_7.strategy_description: Run player forward for significant distance (2000 units). Check number of active world segments to ensure memory isn't leaking (old segments removed).
TEST_7.expected_outcome: Total active path segments should stay within a constant range (e.g., 20-30), proving old segments are despawned and new ones created.
TEST_7.strategy: Run player forward for significant distance (2000 units). Check number of active world segments to ensure memory isn't leaking (old segments removed).
TEST_7.expected: Total active path segments should stay within a constant range (e.g., 20-30), proving old segments are despawned and new ones created.
